reviews.author: BowlCut_Cade
reviews.content:
Love the design of the app and the timer function. There are UI/UX issues that drive me a little crazy that may be simple fixes:

I like dragging scheduled events within the day to help me quickly reschedule, but the timeline is so wonky when trying to do this. An example is if I have an event scheduled at 2 pm that I want to drag to 3 pm, when I start to drag it, it will start at 12 am. I’d really like to see it be more intuitive in moving times around where it doesn’t feel like I’m trying very hard to drag an event from one time to another.

Another opportunity is the live time notifications. If a task starts and I adjust the time, or even I complete a task with time left, it will show me a timer of the event still on my live time notifications.

The last thing I want to point out is some of the AI optimizations when planning tasks. For example, I made a task to “Plan my day” and it automatically put 9 hours on it.

It may seem like I’m only complaining but I really do enjoy this app. As it improves, I can see this being my most used app by a mile.

reviews.author: Ozmiester
reviews.content: Tiimo AI says it creates tasks and put things on my calendar, but it doesn’t. Biggest waste of money.

I really wanted to like this app, but it ended up being a frustrating waste of money.

I was charged $9 for a service that simply didn’t work for me. During the free trial, I reached out to support multiple times because I honestly thought I might be doing something wrong (I genuinely wanted to give the app a fair chance). The main problem was that the AI assistant never synced all of my calendars correctly. My schedule looked messy and inconsistent, with information scattered everywhere, and I couldn’t trust what I was seeing.

I even let the subscription renew because I figured the payment might fix a registration issue or unlock proper syncing. It didn’t. Today I gave it another try and asked the planner to schedule tasks within specific time windows, in a specific order, with clear priorities. It wouldn’t load. It kept freezing, showing errors, or generating tasks incorrectly.

That’s when I decided to cancel and stop using it. The AI assistant is basically the reason I downloaded the app, and it didn’t help me plan at all. Honestly, I get more useful planning help from ChatGPT than from this app.

I can’t recommend paying for this unless the calendar sync and AI reliability are seriously improved.

reviews_history.title: Area for improvement AHAD
reviews_history.content:
I have ADHD, and I would have appreciated being able to modify the order of tasks in the schedule — for example, on Friday, moving a task from the morning to the afternoon simply by dragging it, or rearranging the order. Right now, you can select a task and drag it, but it doesn’t actually move anywhere.

It would also be great to have a comprehensive tutorial, as well as a list of prompts that could help us use the AI more easily.

That’s the only improvement I would suggest. Otherwise, the app is complete.

I look forward to your response for 5 star. I’m happy to be using your application and to have subscribed to the premium plan.

P.S. Your app could truly take off if you focused on usage blogs with practical information about time management and how to use Tiimo in the most optimal way. That would really help people who struggle like I do to have access to lessons and tips to improve personally. It would also give you real added value compared to your competitors. And don’t forget to include guidance about free time in those resources — because thanks to you (or maybe because of you!), I’ve become a real workaholic.

Kiss for the team 💛

reviews.content: Cette app n’est pas au point. Elle offre une interface dépassée, argue proposer un assistant pour « TDAH ». A mon avis ils n’y connaissent rien au TDAH. Mais je vais leur donner une piste de travail, qui devrait valoir services de consultants : un TDAH requiert d’une app qu’elle assume une interface avec le moins possible de manipulations et le moins possible de sous-menus. Visiblement, cette app a été construite à l’envers : un maximum de manipulations, un maximum de sous menus. L’IA n’apporte rien, dans ce contexte, sauf de la cosmétique. Reflechissez sérieusement à votre modèle, car, glaner des prix « idéologiques » ou falsifier les commentaires ne mène à long termes nulle part.

reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Je révise ma note à la baisse. Cette application, primée par Apple, prône l’inclusif et le ramadan. Pourquoi pas ?  Toutefois, avant de proposer aux minorités un outil s’inscrivant dans la dialectique de leurs croyances, il serait judicieux d’offrir à la majorité un outil fiable et fonctionnel. Hors, proposer une IA labellisée « agenda », est un doux euphémisme, car ce qui fonctionne dans Tiimo, c’est les jolies icônes, le chat avec l’IA (très chouette). En revanche ce qui ne fonctionne pas dans Tiimo, c’est l’interface, et l’organisation. Alors messieurs les « programmeurs », avant de penser inclusivité, pensez majorité, et n’oubliez pas a la  prochaine réunion dev d’entreprise de discuter des points soulevés par mon commentaire. Votre application est très en retard sur les standards d’interface de la concurrence. Une IA oui, à la marge pour le chat c’est fun, une appli qui offre de vraies fonctionnalités et non un beau discours marketing, ce serait mieux. Merci.

reviews_history.title: Appli buggée, simple IA
reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Je mets 2 étoiles à cette app car il ne s’agit ni d’une app pour TDAH, ni d’une APP fonctionnelle tout court, pour s’organiser. C’est ni plus ni moins qu’une « ia ». Les fonctionnalités présentées comme « planning, rappel, todo » ne sont ni calibrées, ni adaptées à un TDAH. Le jour où l’équipe de développement se décidera à créer une interface basée sur des l’API / framework de drag and drop, le jour ou l’équipe dev unifiera ses fonctionnalités, dans une interface qui réponde à une LOGIQUE TDAH, le jour ou l’équipe dev testera son appli pour en expurger ses bugs, nous pourrons croire que Tiimo s’avère une appli légitime et non un projet téléphoné. D’ailleurs, le prix reçu démontre à lui seul combien ceux qui couronnent une appli, sont de parfaits ignares en matière de tests/connaissances techniques. Ils ne font qu’élire une « étiquette  » idéologique (TDAH-diversitaire) plus qu’une réussite industrielle à proprement parler.

L’application est un nid à bugs qui n’ont jamais été corrigés. Exemple simple, le timer. Lorsqu’une tâche est terminée, il continue à tourner dans le vide (écran verrouillé et îlot). L’application en elle-même est basée sur une interface des années 2010, l’IA n’apporte aucune valeur ajoutée. Il suffit de comparer les avancées d’appli comme Structured avec Tiimo pour se rendre compte que le niveau en graphic design n’a strictement rien à voir. Cette app a gagnée subitement des notes alors qu’elle plafonnait à 3.9, on se pose la question des faux avis. Toujours est il qu’elle ne règle aucuns des problèmes, elle propose une interface has been, elle intègre une ia qui n’apporte aucune valeur ajoutée.
reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.title: Appli buggée, simple IA

reviews_history.content: J’ai pris un abonnement 1 an, je regrette. Cette application est mal pensée. Elle résonne «  on mets une ia et ça fera le job ». Sauf que l’ergonomie n’est pas votre soucis. Ajouter des « + » de partout, ne rendre aucune rubrique interchangeable par « copier-coller » = 1980. Ainsi, vous proposez une interface des années 1980 avec comme teaser : « nous avons une IA révolutionnaire  ». Pourquoi ne pas sérieusement refondre l’interface, réfléchir sérieusement à son ergonomie dans le but de la rendre en adéquation avec les standards ergonomiques de 2025, puis ensuite, parlez nous d’IA. Votre application en l’état n’est autre qu’une IA flanquée d’une camisole de force. C’est dommage.

reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Toujours aucuns progrès véritables. Pourquoi une fiche « à faire » puis dans l’agenda « matin, midi, soir » ? Pourquoi les événements « n’importe quand » ne sont pas « tirables » sous glissé déposé dans les rubriques « matin-midi-soir » ? Pourquoi le bouton oranger « + » ne peut pas être tiré jusqu’à une rubrique de l’onglet agenda ? Pourquoi les stats n’indiquent rien sur les heures passées à se concentrer, à la semaine, au mois ? Pourquoi remanier 200 fois l’interface pour aboutir à déstabiliser les utilisateurs ? Votre application pourrait être intéressante, hors, vous prenez une direction contre productive.

reviews_history.title: L’app qui donne des BOUTONS…

reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Votre appli est une usine à gaz. Vous agrégez des fonctions  sans en tester l’ergonomie générale. Résultat, un cloaque de fonctionnalités pas possible. Ajouter des tâches demande 6 entrées (+, bouton ia, bouton classique etc). Pas très logique. Vous observez la concurrence, pourquoi prendre ce qu’il y a de plus mauvais et laisser le meilleur ? Je ne comprends toujours pas pourquoi la reconnaissance des dates, mois, années, ne fait pas partie du bouton classique. Je ne comprends pas pourquoi le bouton classique d’ajout de tâches ne se « tire » pas jusqu’au créneau souhaité pour directement renseigner l’heure. Je ne comprends pas pourquoi « anytime » rubrique sous le planning ne permets pas de « déplacer » par glisser-déposer une tâche dans le planning. Vous préférez les clics et étapes multiples. Quand je suis devant votre app, la simple pensée de devoir saisir une entrée me rebute. On a pas tous l’envie de passer par l’IA, ia qui d’ailleurs demande à renseigner des prompts précis pour aboutir au bon discernement de l’IA. Pareil, à quoi sert la liste todo ? Quand je « tire » une tâche et la place sur l’icône du bas « aujourd’hui », manque de bol, ça ne fonctionne pas. Et non, il faut encore passer par le menu, 50 clics ici, 50 clics par là. Franchement, à l’heure des app structured, Todoist, l’heure n’est plus aux sous-sous-sous menus. L’heure est aux copier-coller, glissés-déposés. D’autres part, je trouve que le design perds en finesse. La page todo et ses grands blocs pointillés ? 1980 ! La barre violette dans la section planning ? 1980 ! Voyez là encore Structured. L’intégration est transparente. À la limite, mettez en gras les horaires visés. Autre problème : quand vous supprimez une tâche, elle continue à tourner dans l’îlot de l’iPhone, la zone des caméras qui s’anime. Une tâche fantôme, qu’il faut aller virer dans la page verrouillée. Ça fait X temps que ce bug ennuie tout le monde. Pourquoi les widgets ne permettent pas d’aboutir à la page planning plutôt que d’aboutir toujours sur le focus ? Pour les stats, j’ai cru comprendre que c’était dans les cartons. Revoyez votre interface, ne cloisonnez pas vos rubriques, rendez les communicantes, plutôt que d’en passer par des menus à rallonges pour réaliser une pauvre action.

reviews_history.content: Cette appli a du potentiel… gâché par les choix faits par l’équipe. 1/ le design s’effondre. Qu’est ce que c’est que cette barre violette horrible et disgracieuse dans la section « planning » ? Wtf ! Voyez structured pour l’exemple d’implémentation du glissé-déposé. Ensuite, qu’est ce que c’est que ces rectangles horribles dans la section To-Do ?? WTF ?! C’est extrêmement disgracieux et cheap 2/ le glissé-déposé (planning) ne permets pas de déposer l’événement dans « anytime » par glissé-déposé ??!! C’est La BASE ! sinon à quoi sert l’implémentation d’un glissé-déposé ? Ensuite : pourquoi les tâches (todo) ne sont pas programmées pour être glissées-déposées en les « déposants »  sur les deux onglets (à faire, aujourd’hui) ? Il faut passer par la fenêtre contextuelle de la tâche, sélectionner dans la liste (todo, at time, anytime etc). Une perte de temps ! Pourquoi 2 boutons de créations de tâches, l’un iA, l’autre classique ?? Vous ne pouvez pas fusionner ? Pourquoi les prompts ne reconnaissent toujours pas les heures, dates dans la création manuelle de tâches ? Incorporer l’Ia, c’est bien, mais, dans quel but ? Prenons un exemple : les sous-taches. Le résultat est totalement useless. Dernier point : il y a un bug sur l’écran de verrouillage. Lorsque l’on efface une tache, elle reste comme une tache « fantôme » à continuer de décompter le temps. Quand ces problèmes seront réglés, je considérerais qu’elle devient indispensable.
reviews_history.author: filouxee

reviews_history.content: Le principal problème de cette application c’est le manque d’ergonomie. 1/ Les RDV de la « ligne de temps » ne sont pas capables d’être déplacés par glisser-déposé. Ensuite, si je créer un RDV « n’importe quand », impossible de « glisser-déposer » dans la ligne de temps. Autre point noir : quand j’écris dans ma création de RDV « faire la lessive à 13h30 », l’app ne reconnaît pas l’heure, hors, ce serait un gain immense. Pour terminer, les stats de l’appli sont useless. Un TDAH requiert une rétrospective de son temps passé. C’est non seulement primordial pour la motivation mais intéressant du point de vue des progres concrets. Dernier point : cliquer ou toucher le widget renvoie immanquablement vers le timer. C’est extrêmement frustrant de devoir ensuite cliquer sur l’onglet planning. Prévoir dans les réglages du widget la possibilité d’aboutir soit sur le timer, soit sur la fenêtre du planning. Lorsque ces points seront considérés, je monterais la note.

reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Cette app était bien mais elle évolue d’une façon qui ne me satisfait pas. De nature TDAH, je dois valoriser le TEMPS passé à travaillé, étant donné que le temps est la notion du TDAH PHARE. Hors, Tiimo ne propose qu’un ersatz de stats. « 2 tâches effectuées ». Super… Nous avons besoin de la donnée du temps passé qui est valorisante. Pourquoi ne pas intégrer, des stats qui renseignent sur le temps passé par types de tâches ? À la limite indiquer « 2 tâches » et « temps de concentration ». Il est crucial de revoir cet aspect de l’appli afin d’offrir une expérience post journée qui soit réellement satisfaisante. Hors en l’état, le boulot se trouve fait à moitié. Okey qu’il est sympa de proposer des outils de suivis. Mais le suivis consiste également en un bilan. Si je dois utiliser une app tierce pour obtenir les infos de temps passés « à », vous échouez dans votre accompagnement. Capturer le temps est LA NOTION phare du TDAH. Procurez lui une complète satisfaction du temps passé à travailler. Je monterais la note lorsque ce paramètre sera intégré. Sinon je laisserai tomber cette appli pourtant prometteuse.

Parler d’IA est également très présomptueux. C’est du gadget. Pour le reste, des bonnes idées quand même. Je mets 5 étoiles pour le moment.

reviews_history.author: filouxee
reviews_history.content: Je viens de Structured et certaines choses me manquent. Ainsi que l’app « focus ». Tout d’abord, il serait bien de proposer une reconnaissance des horaires. « Manger à 8h pendant 30mn ». Todoist le fait remarquablement. Ensuite, pouvoir déplacer les taches pour les réordonner au doigt mais surtout les passer de la section « a tout moment » vers « plannifié ». Pour finir, des statistiques seraient importantes pour quantifier le temps passé par activités ou sur une activité. Dernière chose, le rimer visuel, ce serait bien de pouvoir avancer le temps si on a été plus vite que prévu sans forcément boucler la tâche. Et les jours du planning, pouvoir faire défiler comme des pages de livre plutôt que de cliquer sur L(lundi) M(mardi) etc. N’oublions pas que le TDAH requiert le moins d’étapes possibles. Merci.

reviews.content_translated: Not up to scratch This app is not up to scratch. It offers an outdated interface, argues that it offers an assistant for "ADHD". In my opinion, they don't know anything about ADHD. But I'll give them a line of work, which should be worth the services of consultants: ADHD requires an app to interface with as few manipulations as possible and as few submenus as possible. Obviously, this app was built in reverse: a maximum of manipulations, a maximum of submenus. AI brings nothing, in this context, except cosmetics. Think seriously about your model, because gleaning "ideological" awards or falsifying comments leads nowhere.

reviews.content_translated: Useless and frustrating And to think that it seemed so promising that it pushed me to subscribe right away. The internal artificial intelligence is absolutely useless, programmed to always give the same two answers (often not in Italian) and does not understand anything of what you write to it, really frustrating. I don't understand what exactly ADHD is supposed to help me with, if not mark things to do, like any other app? I regret spending this money and time to try it.

reviews.content: E pensare che sembrava così promettente da spingermi a fare l’abbonamento subito.. Non si capisce in cosa esattamente dovrebbe aiutarmi come ADHD, se non segnare le cose da fare, come qualsiasi altra app? Rimpiango di aver speso questi soldi e tempo per provarla.

reviews.content:
I’m a couple of days into my trial and just debating whether to subscribe or not…..my initial thoughts…..firstly this is pretty close to the planner/calendar/to-do list app that I seem to constantly be searching for but there are a few little details that are making me pause…..
- Firstly, I have a million to do’s for different areas of my life and there isn’t an easy way to categorise them, you can colour code them (although there isn’t a way to save your favourite colours so this is a bit laborious). I’ve grouped them by colour all the high/medium/low priority tasks but wish there was a way to filter by type so I could just see Life tasks or work etc if I chose - for this reason I find myself still using Blitzit at the same time as this app. 
- On the web version, unless you can see the high priority section you can’t drag from the generic to do list (it doesn’t auto scroll up) so I have to drop in low list, then move to medium then finally to the high priority list - not a huge deal but makes it all feel a bit less fluid plus I’d forget which task I was moving and it’s all got a bit jumbled re-checking the priority lists 
- the ability to add subtasks to a task would be excellent, another reason I find myself still using Blitzit simultaneously - otherwise my lists become hugely unmanageable listing all of the subtasks rather than one overall task. 
- I wish there was an option for the week view (even if only when in full screen view) to have the same layout as the 3-day view (which is perfect with anytime/morning/afternoon and evening!) but the week view just has generic calendar columns and only stretches the day columns wider instead - it’s would be so much more enjoyable to use if you could have the 3-day layout in the week view
- I work a couple of different jobs, including one that is shift work, I dream of a calendar that I can allocate a colour to each day in the month view to represent what type of day it is at a glance. I think I’ve managed a work around by adding my shift type to the ‘anytime’ tasks for each day in the 3-day view with a different colour for each, and I realise this is perhaps an unrealistic ask but that’s my calendar unicorn so I forever live in hope. 

Ultimately, it’s a great app super close to what I’ve been hunting for but I think I will probably still need to use other apps alongside - I can live without the different colours for days but the to do list categories filters, subtasks and weirdly enough, not having the 3-day view layout as an option for the week view might just be dealbreakers, I’m not sure just yet….will see how this week goes

I was originally very excited to discover this app — the accolades and hype made it appear to be the productivity and task tracking tool I had been searching for. At first, it does seem great — the core workflow of brain dumping tasks then scheduling feels very natural and smooth — but after just a few days the bugs and usability red flags begin to show. Below are some examples:

The freemium model is unnecessarily restrictive. With the free version you can create a task, but you need to pay $50+ a year if you want to edit that task.

The AI mode is borderline useless. You ask for help managing tasks and it will spit out a list of all of your tasks. I asked it to create a task called “call dentist office to schedule appointment” and had to go back and forth with the chat it because it ignored the ask and tried to actually create the appointment itself. It also isn’t clear from the chat output whether it actually adds something to your schedule or to your todo as you are always promoted to add a task after AI output..very confusing.

The focus mode is extremely buggy. There is no way to cancel a focus mode. If you have a scheduled task it automatically starts a focus state at that time and you can’t change that preference, which means anything you sync from your calendar or reminders will likely auto start and you will spend your time juggling pausing/unpausing or completing tasks and unchecking to get things to the correct state. You can drag the circle all the way around to complete a focus ring but you can’t drag it part of the way around to speed it up?

Tasks have focus rings but not sub tasks so managing time when there are many sub tasks is a fools errand. 

The best part of the app is what is offered for free. If basic free features weren’t gated by the freemium model, I could see the value in this app because aesthetically it’s probably the best one out there. If you strip away most of what is advertised as “pro” features (AI, lock screen live updates, widgets), I’d be happy with a todo + schedule and reminder notifications and would pay for a lesser subscription cost or lifetime fee.

reviews.title: 그냥 ADHD이름만 단…
reviews.content_translated:
Just ADHD name... If you talk to a real ADHD patient who has been using it for over a month, just write a reminder and a calendar. And take advantage of shortcuts and automations.
If there is a difference in this app, it is a beautiful screen
It's the same, but it doesn't help my symptoms. 
Above all, the app is heavy. I'm using an iPhone 17 Pro and it's confusing that it loads before deciding on an action. It's not that you have to do the work level of other professional productive AI, and there is no part of every action that doesn't need to be loaded. I'd rather write on the web.
 The reason for the existence of AI is also vague, and the most disappointing part is the Korean language recognition part. As of February 26, there is really nothing I know how to do when comparing the performance of most AIs.   Perhaps the fact that my Korean recognition skills are very low also plays a role. 
If you can't read the way ADHD is spoken, it's not just a pity to sell it with the name ADHD. And the spectrum of ADHD is not just a variable, but a variable that has to be done and this and this in a conversation with AI, right? Even if the two-week routine is clearly a keyword presented in AI chat, if you ask me, I would appreciate it if you could read the sentence and distribute one cell for each event if you just wrote it in a sub-task for an event. Is it true that you have experienced or studied ADHD? 
Even if you enter it yourself, the event time or period settings are not a little different from other apps, but the existing custom range setting part is insufficient and inconvenient. 
It's a one-year subscription, so you can think it's a few thousand won a month, but it's not worth it even if you compare it with my financial situation and other tools. If the tool bought for function is only pretty on the shell, it is... Yes.
As I continue to use it, there are not one or two regrets, but I'm glad that the advance notification and calendar are linked. - If that's the case, why should I buy this app? It comes down to one question. 
First of all, the developer will not release it in Korea
It seems that there was no more than a lack of review of language experience packs and research on professional ADHD symptoms. 
I hope this review was delivered to the developer in an accurate translation, and the existing user reviews are reviews of general users of the good-looking app with ADHD name or not.
Indistinguishable 
I really don't recommend it to people who really have problems with time management. 
For those whose lives have changed with this app, you will want to study the life before this app.
reviews.answer_text: 안녕하세요, jambiu님! 사용해 주셔서 감사합니다. 귀하의 소중한 피드백을 통해 앱의 실제 사용 경험을 알게 되어 매우 감사하게 생각합니다. 말씀하신 대로, 앱의 로딩 속도와 한국어 인식 부분에서 아쉬움을 느끼셨다는 점은 저희에게 중요한 정보입니다. 저희는 ADHD 사용자로서의 귀하의 경험을 깊이 이해하고 있으며, 이러한 피드백은 저희가 더 나은 방향으로 나아가는 데 큰 도움이 됩니다. 앞으로도 사용자분들의 다양한 의견을 소중히 여기며, 더 나은 서비스를 제공하기 위해 노력하겠습니다. 다시 한 번, 귀하의 피드백에 감사드리며, 앞으로의 경험이 더 나아지기를 바랍니다. /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜
reviews.author: jambiu
reviews.content:
한달 넘게 사용해본 진짜 ADHD환자 한명으로 이야기하면 그냥 미리알림과 캘린더를 쓰세요. 그리고 단축어, 자동화기능을 활용하세요.
이 앱의 차별성이라면 예쁜 화면인 것
같은데 제 증상에 도움 되지않고 
무엇보다 앱이 무겁습니다. 저는 iphone 17 pro사용 중인데 액션 결정하기 전 로딩이 의아스러울 정도입니다. 다른 전문 생산형 AI의 작업 수준을 해내라는 것도 아니고 모든 액션에 로딩없이 되는 부분이 하나도 없습니다. 차라리 웹으로 쓰세요.
 AI의 존재의 이유도 희미하며 가장 실망한 부분은 한국어 인식부분입니다. 26년 2월 현재 대부분의 AI의 성능비교시 정말 할 줄 아는게 없습니다.   아마도 한국어 인식능력이 아주 낮다는 것도 한 몫 하는 것 같습니다. 
ADHD의 화법을 못읽으면 ADHD이름을 달고 팔기엔 그냥 아쉬운 수준이 아닙니다. 그리고 ADHD의 스펙트럼이 한가지도 아니고 AI와의 대화에서 이거하고 이거하고 이거해야해만 변수가 아니잖습니까? 2주 루틴이 분명 AI채팅으로 제시된 키워드임에도 부탁하면 한 이벤트에 하위작업에 그냥 본인이 적은 긴 글이 어떠한 언어처리도 없이 한 개의 셀에 적용 되어있거나 다행일 땐 문장을 읽고 한 이벤트당 하나의 셀을 배분해주면 다행입니다. 개발자는 ADHD를 경험해보거나 연구한게 맞나요? 
직접 입력하더라도 이벤트시간 혹은 기간 설정도 기타 다른 앱과 조금 다르고 다름의 이유가 아니라 기존 커스텀 범위 설정부분이 부족하고 불편합니다. 
1년권이어서 달 몇천원인데 생각할 수 있지만 제 재정상황과 여타 도구들과 비교에 빼더라도 값어치를 못합니다. 기능을 위해 산 도구가 껍데기만 예쁘면 그것은… 네.
계속 쓰면서 아쉬운게 한두개가 아닌데 그나마 미리알림과 캘린더가 연동되는건 다행이나- 그럴꺼면 이 앱을 왜 구매해야하나의 질문으로 다시 귀결됩니다. 
일단 개발자는 한국에 출시전에
언어경험팩의 검수와 전문 ADHD 증상에 대한 연구가 부족이라는 표현 이상으로 없었던 걸로 밖에 보이지 않습니다. 
이 리뷰가 정확한 번역으로 개발자에게 전해졌음 좋겠고 기존 사용자 리뷰들은 ADHD이름의 보기좋은 어플의 일반사용자들의 리뷰인지 아닌지
구별할 수 없는 바로 
정말 시간관리에 문제가 있는 증상의 분들께는 정말로 추천하지 않습니다. 
이 어플로 인생이 바꼈다는 분의 이 앱이전의 삶을 연구하고 싶어질 지경입니다.

reviews.content:
Ich durfte Tiimo nun für ein paar Tage kostenfrei probieren. Im Vergleich mit  anderen Tages-Planungs-App‘s hat Tiimo meiner Meinung nach den besten Ansatz. 
Leider ist die technische Umsetzung aktuell so fehlerhaft, dass eine langfristige Nutzung für mich aktuell nicht in Frage kommt. Dazu gehören Punkte wie eine KI, die in meiner Sprache antwortet,  importierte Erinnerungen, die sich nicht bei Import vervielfachen (eigentlich sollten diese Synchronisiert werden, nicht nur importiert), Kalender die korrekt synchronisiert werden (inklusive Wegzeit) und eine Zeitleiste, die ich wirklich per Drag and Drop umstrukturieren kann (ohne dass meine Aufgaben mit einer Startzeit von 00:00 Uhr versehen werden). 
Meiner Meinung nach ist es sehr schade, dass all diese technischen Probleme diese App unbrauchbar machen. Von den Ideen her, mit dem Aufgaben Timer, der lockeren Tagesunterteilung Ohne feste Zeiten und vielen anderen Dingen, ist es nämlich die von vielversprechendste App die ich bisher getestet hab. 
PS: Bitte antwortet nur, wenn Ihr etwas produktives zu sagen habt. Ich benötige keine Antwort, wenn es nur die Standard Antwort von einem Bot ist, der hofft das ich in Zukunft 5 Sterne geben werde.
reviews.content_translated:
Great ideas, bad implementation I was allowed to try Tiimo for free for a few days. Compared to other day planning apps, Tiimo has the best approach in my opinion. 
Unfortunately, the technical implementation is currently so flawed that long-term use is currently out of the question for me. These include points like an AI that answers in my language, imported reminders that don't multiply on import (actually, these should be synchronized, not just imported), calendars that are synchronized correctly (including travel time) and a timeline that I can really restructure via drag and drop (without my tasks being given a start time of 00:00). 
In my opinion, it's a shame that all these technical issues make this app unusable. In terms of ideas, with the task timer, the loose daily division without fixed times and many other things, it is the most promising app I have tested so far. 
PS: Please only answer if you have something productive to say. I don't need an answer if it's just the standard answer from a bot that hopes I'll give 5 stars in the future.

reviews.author: lizdexandivy
reviews.content:
I have tried lots of planners, but they always seem usable for a day or two, and then - the next thing you know - you realise that you haven’t used it for a week…

I had the version with the AI integration, and I think this is what makes it work so well. By asking it to create a task, you need to give it some details about the task itself… then you can ask it to create subtasks. <— this is where the magic happens. By breaking it down into potential tasks that are close enough to make you think about what they actually need to be, you end up properly planning the detailed steps and checking them off one by one. 

This has been massive for me. I’ve been using it for a month - for work tasks and basic everyday tasks and it’s a game changer. 

It’s especially great if your someone who humps from one task to another like I do. ADHDers, this one’s a dream!
reviews.answer_text: Hey lizdexandivy! It's fantastic to hear how well the app has been working for you! We're so grateful that the AI integration and task breakdown feature have truly made a difference in your productivity. Your feedback means a lot to us! /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜

reviews.content:
I have autism and ADHD, and trying to keep myself organised has always been a huge issue for me. I’d tried planners and to-do lists in the past, but I’d never had much luck.

When I began my Master’s degree, I was struggling so badly with organisation that I decided to start looking at apps. When I saw that Tiimo was designed with neurodivergence in mind, I decided to give it a try.

It’s nearly February now, and I still use Tiimo every single day. It feels intuitive to me, and has been incredibly helpful.

Five stars!!

reviews.content:
I see the potential for a great app here, but weird details pull it back from working for me.

Some examples:
- Your able to drag tasks on the To-Do tab to assign priority, but you can’t drag do assign a time of day on Calendar.

- Scheduling a task from the To-Do list to the calendar removes its priority ranking. Priority doesn’t exist in the calendar view.

- The AI struggles to break down my speech into goals. If I say a list of things but it gets one wrong and I tell it to fix it, the whole other list gets ditched and need to start over.

- The educational content is riddled with scientific speak, which makes it hard to follow. Also the host in the videos I watched is clearly reading from a teleprompter which is very distracting.

Overall, I don’t like it that I payed for the pro without trying the free version first. There is potential here but too many quirks prevent it from truly being a game changer.

reviews.title: Great interface, needs work
reviews.content:
The app is beautiful - good colors, organization, and satisfying UI. However the actual features need much improvement. I bought an annual sub of this app years ago, but it just wasn’t there yet. I came back for it today, just because I do miss the app and wanted to see how much has changed.

1) DRAG & DROP feature on timeline is janky. It is not intuitive. If I have a task scheduled for 4pm, and want to move a different task to right after it, nope. Whatever invisible timeline the spacing is set on is where the task will go. I could move the task after the 4pm one, but it will move to 2am or 3am. I need to keep pulling the task lower and lower, until the small times on left show the correct time, and it could be past 11pm tasks. It is confusing and does not make sense, and sometimes I forget what exact time I want it scheduled at, i just know i wanted it after my other scheduled task! 

2) Would also like a feature to drag duration of tasks, instead of needing to click into it and edit. Too time consuming.

3) What is the point of adding minutes focus time if it doesn’t shift the events after it? Let’s say i have a task with a 30 minute duration. I go over by 7 minutes. Now the rest of my schedule is messed up, obviously i cannot start on my next task if im still focusing on the current one, and i’ll get multiple notifications during my current one, alerting me of upcoming tasks or to start them, when I am 7 minutes behind!! it is overwhelming and ruins the purpose of the focus. They really need to create an automatic shift feature. 

4)  AI chat is useless right now, simply not there yet. I hate how it is at the bottom right, where the most useful button should be, and the new task feature is all the way on the top. Need to switch these two. 

I want this app to work well SOOO badly and have gone back and forth waiting for it to get where it needs to be, but I think this may be the last time I download if these features cannot be updated.
reviews.answer_text: Hi T1ffyT! It's wonderful to hear that you appreciate the beautiful interface and organization of the app! reviews.content:
The assistant is so clunky. And as a persistent brain dumper, it would be so helpful if it could actually do what it says it can. It constantly misunderstands what I’m asking for, and will often ignore 80% of my message and just create a single task I don’t want. I was hoping it would be more intelligent at listing tasks and estimating how long they actually take. Anyways, LOVE the auto prioritization as mentioned and the calendar integration. I’ll keep it for the free trial but I’m not convinced to go pro with it yet.

reviews_history.content:
As a **premium subscriber** for over a year, I've witnessed the evolution of this app from a sleek, efficient tool to a **bloated, hard-to-navigate mess**. Let me break down my experience:

1. **Initial Impressions:**
   - The app used to be a joy to use—minimalistic, responsive, and reliable.
   - Unfortunately, recent updates have shifted the focus away from functionality and toward **monetization**.

2. **Revenue Streams Over User Experience:**
   - It's evident that the developers are trying to squeeze more revenue out of the app.
   - New features seem to prioritize **classes and body doubling** (whatever that means) rather than improving the core functionality.
   -This stinks of an equity firm buy out… and we all know how that ends up

3. **Technical Issues:**
   - **Watch complications** stopped working months ago, leaving me frustrated.
   - Now, the **widgets** are also malfunctioning, and guess what? They require a premium subscription—- BUT I HAVE ONE…hrmmmm
   - Worse still, I have to **restore my purchases daily** just to keep the app running. Sometimes, even an **uninstall and reinstall** is necessary.

4. **Price Hike:**
   - The most baffling part is the **price increase**. What justifies the jump from a reasonable $30/year to a whopping $100?
   - I've scoured the app, but I can't find any groundbreaking additions that warrant such a steep cost.

5. **Developer Greed:**
   - It's disheartening to see developers prioritize profits over user satisfaction.
   - **Fabulous app** fell into the same trap, and now it seems this one is following suit.

6. **Moving On:**
   - I've decided to switch to **Structured**, where affordability and functionality still matter.
   - Farewell, bloated app—I'm off to greener pastures unless you can fix these issues before renewal.
reviews_history.author: 72bleh33
reviews_history.title: From Slim to Bloated: A Disappointing Transformation
reviews_history.answer_text:
Hi, thanks for your very detailed review and we are sorry to hear that we have taken away the nice experience that you had when you first started using the app. Please know that we are now in the process of simplifying the app a little to make it easy to use. Our hope is that this makes for a better user experience and more easy navigation of the app. We are sorry to hear that you have been experiencing technical issues in the app- we are already working on fixing the watch widget bug and we hope to have the fix out soon. Please be on a look out soon or reach out and give us your contact details so that we can let you know when the fix is available, thanks. We will also like to look into the widget issue that you have mentioned, thanks.


Kindly be aware that the new features in the Learn tab were introduced after a lot of data was gathered which showered that a lot of our users are not looking only for a planning tool, but they are also looking for more ways to navigate their different neurodiversity diagnose and connect with like-minded people who understand what they are going through. There is a difference in the prices based on whether one selects Tiimo Plan (the cheaper subscription) or Tiimo Learn (the higher subscription as it has both planning features and learn features- and expert courses that are offered by coaches that we have to pay at the end of the day - they don´t work for free).


Thanks again for your review and feedback and please know that all the changes we make in the app are to support our users, which has always been our mission since when we first introduced our app. Warmly, Team Tiimo

reviews.content:
I’ve been struggling with routines and forgetting about everything as one does with ADHD/I. Usually whenever I try an app to help with routines, I start strong, then quickly get irritated by its frequent and overbearing reminders and end up uninstalling it. This app has finally broken through that 2 week long window and has become a part of my day to day. Notifications are not overbearing and it integrates smoothly with your iPhone. The AI is very helpful and allows you to brain dump everything and creates tasks for you. It syncs with all of your calendars and reminders and puts it all in one place.

However, I do wish that the app would remove items completed in subtasks for “main tasks”. I also wish that you could take a step further and create subtasks for your subtasks. Granted these are minor inconveniences.

Love the app!
reviews.answer_text: Hey That One Dude Tom! We're so glad to hear that the app has become a helpful part of your daily routine! It's fantastic that you've found the right balance with notifications and appreciate how seamlessly it integrates with your calendar. Your feedback about subtasks is super valuable, and we're thrilled to know you're enjoying the overall experience. Thank you for sharing your thoughts! /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜

reviews.content: I just found this app after trying quite a few in hopes of finding a scheduler that allowed me to create sub-tasks within a task on my to-do list. For a long time now I’ve been making daily to-do lists on the notes app but Tiimo has been a game changer for me with some features I didn’t know I needed. Keeping in mind I haven’t been trying it for long, so far it’s been good with only a couple small hiccups. I love being able to use natural language to have Tiimo create items in my calendar. I did have a couple occasions where it replied to me in a different language for some reason but it then continued in English after I questioned it. I also tried telling it to remind me of a list of peoples birthdays each year, which I provided and for some reason one of them it recited back to me with the incorrect date. It was simple to fix but enough to withhold a full 5 star rating. That said, I am so happy I found this app and look forward to future improvements. As somebody with ADHD and anxiety it feels nice to have assistance with knowing what I should prioritize each day to feel like I’ve been productive enough and can continue to work on myself while maintaining a professional schedule.

reviews.author: Shawnx12240
reviews.content:
Don’t get me wrong the concept of the app is great. Without paying for AI Features it’s just another notes app with a todo list format with AI. The free version is the best this app gets without massive headaches. When you pay so you can have the planner to plan out your day and such problems arise. I thankfully went into this free trial I got knowing from some fairly recent experience how awful AI can’t be at managing a list depending on what certain actions you may request. But then there was glitchiness at times as well as just a pure inability to do simpler tasks like be sure to schedule in a very open morning the task not overlapping something else. I spent a lot more time in the app planning and fighting with the AI to do what I asked then I ever should have.

I’d really like to see something like this succeed but it’s my opinion that making us have to pay for such experimental features is awful, although at the same time I understand the need for a subscription service, but I don’t know what to suggest instead. Because it seems at least ChatGPT has issues with list maybe another AI if that’s what your using or just work towards solutions and call your AI as ai highly suggest “experimental” because that is what it is. Bad at lists or not I made similar requests to ChatGPT directly and it did it far better job then the app. 

I think I’ll just use my $20 a month for ChatGPT that I already pay for anyway to do this apps job. I’ll have half the issues. A headache still, but oh well. 

I really hope one day this AI can feel much more capable and so can use this or another app like it because when things went well it was really nice to use
reviews.answer_text: Hi Shawnx12240! It’s clear that your experience with the AI features has been challenging, and we appreciate you sharing your thoughts. We recognize that the app's AI can sometimes struggle with tasks like scheduling, which can lead to frustration. Your feedback on the need for improvement in this area is invaluable as we strive to enhance the user experience. We’re thankful for your understanding of the subscription model, and we truly hope that our AI can evolve and provide a smoother experience in the future. Your insights are essential in guiding us toward making this app better, and we’re glad to hear that when things worked well, you enjoyed using it. We hope for 5 stars next time! Thank you for your thoughtful feedback! /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜

reviews.content:
Net begonnen op advies van revalidatie (NAH): Eigenlijk een hele handige app die voor mij intuïtief werkt. 

Wat ik wel mis is:
- dat de taken niet op alfabetische volgorde kunnen (ik verplaats ze nu zelf één voor één.
- dat er geen labels gekoppeld kunnen worden (bv Huis/Werk etc), zodat het geordender voor het hoofd is.
- dat het niet goed in te schatten is wat belangrijk/urgent is zonder daarvoor de hele lijst af te lopen, en dat is bij mij (en ik denk anderen ook) een flinke chaotische hoofd-lijst.

Wat ik super vind is:
- dat er bij een taak zelf opties voor tussenstappen kunnen worden gegeven. 
- dat ik eerst m’n hoofd kan leegooien en daarna kan plannen. Als er met de bovenstaande punten iets kan zou dat heel fijn zijn.
- dat er handige snelle uitleg-filmpjes in zitten. 
- dat ik nu van allemaal verschillende apps alles in één app kan (bij)houden.
- het uiterlijk ziet er leuk uit en de herinneringen zijn absoluut niet opdringerig maar helpend (voor mij).
- en vast nog meer maar dat moet ik nog ontdekken.

In vergelijking met andere apps vind ik, ondanks dat het wel wat kost voor alle mogelijkheden (heb de lifetime versie), deze wel de app die ik blijvend wil gaan gebruiken. Leuk! Dank voor het maken!
reviews.answer_text: Hallo Edjelf, Wat fijn om te horen dat de app intuïtief werkt en je er al veel voordelen uit haalt! We waarderen je suggesties enorm en zijn blij dat je de mogelijkheid om taken op te splitsen en de uitlegvideo’s handig vindt. Jouw feedback over organisatie-inzichten, zoals alfabetische sortering en labels, is waardevol en we danken je voor het delen van deze punten. We hopen dat je ons de volgende keer een 5-sterren beoordeling kunt geven! Geniet verder van het gebruiksgemak en het mooie ontwerp! /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜
reviews.author: Edjelf
reviews.content_translated:
Well, with a few points Just started on the advice of rehabilitation (ABI): Actually a very handy app that works intuitively for me. 

What I do miss is:
- that the tasks cannot be arranged alphabetically (I am now moving them one by one myself.
- that no labels can be linked (e.g. Home/Work etc), so that it is more orderly for the head.
- that it is not easy to assess what is important/urgent without going through the entire list, and that is a big chaotic main list for me (and I think others too).

What I think is great:
- that options for intermediate steps can be given for a task itself. 
- that I can first throw my head and then plan. If something can be done with the above points, that would be very nice.
- that there are handy quick explanation videos in it. 
- that I can now keep track of all kinds of different apps in one app.
- The appearance looks nice and the memories are absolutely not intrusive but helpful (for me).
- and probably more but I still have to discover that.

Compared to other apps, despite the fact that it costs a bit for all the features (have the lifetime version), I think this one is the app I want to use permanently. Nice! Thanks for making!

reviews_history.title: Only gotten worse
reviews_history.content: I’ve had and use to pay for this app for years, I’m AUDHD and this used to be THE app for planning my schedule. Over the years it gotten bogged down with a TON of bloat material. I didn’t need an AI To Do app, I don’t want it to ask me to input my mood, that’s not why I used this app. Where did the pre-saved and pre-made routines go?? Why can the app no longer intuitively input what time to start a task when I’m planning it for after a previous task with a logged time?? Half the time I enter a task and it vanishes. The UI is so busy now. If you wanted to make a To Do list app then make that app separate. I wish I could un-update and go back to the really well program and designed Routine app Tiimo use to be.

reviews.title: Automatic Icons are an eyesore
reviews.author: Mercenary Writer
reviews.content: This app is near perfect, but the automatic icons are turning into a deal-breaker for me. I have to manually remove them every time I create a new task— and leaving them on ruins the look of the app for me. I find them extremely distracting and deeply visually unappealing. For $12 a month, I should be able to turn this feature off. Without that ability, and with the constant usage barrier it presents, I’ll be looking elsewhere. I’ll check back, and hope it changes. I’m so glad I didn’t pay for the annual subscription, or I’d be pretty upset. It didn’t occur to me that this wouldn’t be optional. What a disappointment.
reviews.answer_text: Hi Mercenary Writer! It’s wonderful to hear that you find the app nearly perfect! We truly appreciate your feedback about the automatic icons, and we completely understand how they can be distracting. Your thoughts on having more customization options are really valuable to us, and we’ve taken note of your concerns. Thank you for sharing your insights, and we hope to earn those 5 stars from you next time! /Warmly Team Tiimo 💜
